page: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v6 is a dedicated virtual machine instance belonging to the Dldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

This instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 236 GB of local disk storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included, and the hardware profile lacks a dedicated GPU.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2141.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.32 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations show limitations; the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores fall into the lower percentile. Memory read bandwidth is relatively weak at 36.76 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though write bandwidth remains average at 25.84 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is measured at 7.72 GB/sec, and LLM prompt processing for a 7B model achieves 33.61 tokens/sec.

Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio, the D4lds_v6 offers a cost-efficient option for compute-bound tasks that do not demand large memory footprints. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and entry-level batch processing. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, or highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads due to its memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling constraints.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4lds_v6 is a dedicated compute-oriented virtual machine from the Dldsv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture and running under Microsoft Hyper-V, the processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by high single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, though multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-focused tasks with modest memory requirements. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v6 is a compute-focused instance featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v6 is a 4-vCPU Intel Xeon instance with 8.0 GB RAM and 236 GB local storage, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processing and local storage for memory-light, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 4 vCPUs and 260 MB L3 cache
8.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-optimized 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
236 GB of local disk storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance in text processing and gzip compression
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support for specialized math acceleration
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, light web serving, and basic batch processing
